Okay, so here's the thousand foot overview before we dive in. One, you'll watch the overview videos, which this is the last one or should be the last one. Next, you're going to build the machines, all the software that I just went over, you're going to be building all that into one unit so that it all works together. After you build your machine, you're going to create your ads and so you'll go through the module about how to create your ads, why it's important, etc. And then you will turn on the machine. That is when we actually start turning on traffic to run clicks to the page so that we can convert those clicks into leads. So we'll turn the machine on, which means you'll actually start having lead flow by the end of this module. After that point, if you want to optimize the scale, obviously during the turn on the machine period, we're not going to say go ham during that period. We want to make sure that things are working, they're converting at a good price, etc. But during the optimize and scaling, it will show you how to decide which adsets are performing the way we want them to, which one should be turned off, and then how to scale the ones that are winning so that you can get more for less. After that, with the troubleshooting ad section, which is just showing what to do if XYZ, if the cost is too high, if you're not getting a lot of them, if your account gets shut down, if something gets flagged, all of those types of things that we have already seen. So you don't necessarily need to go there unless you have a trouble that needs to be shot. And then finally, we have FAQ, which is just the most asked questions that we get around lead gen. And then finally, alternate platforms, which in this case will be Instagram, so I'll show you how to run ads on Instagram. Most of the time, you don't need to go there because your ad should be performing just fine on Facebook, given the amount of volume that you probably want and or need. But we will show you how to run Instagram ads if you need more traffic than you have, and the training on that is contained within the last module. So hope that makes sense. And finally, we are going to get into the nitty-gritty and start putting this machine together.
